name: teamwork-skill
description: Codex-first skills (also installable on Cursor and Claude Code) for lean evidence-first collaboration, bounded subagents, review, goal convergence, and opt-in lifecycle sounds.

interface.shortDescription: Deep Codex collaboration with goals, proactive custom agents, plans, and fresh review.
interface.longDescription: A Codex-first broad entrypoint plus a lean explicit grill-me interaction skill, dedicated stages for project instruction initialization, evidence gathering, durable memory, scoped execution, native fast-path handling, bounded role-subagent work, fresh review, package updates, and goal convergence. Material decisions use host-native structured input when callable and concise text otherwise.
defaultPrompt: Design a Codex native goal with evidence, scope, retry policy, artifacts, and subagent routing.
Initialize or slim project AGENTS/CODEX/CLAUDE rules with performance-first or cost-first Codex mode while preserving local evidence boundaries.
Fan out valuable independent Codex work into evidence-gathering, planning, execution, and review tracks.
Ground facts, classify ownership, and ask only unresolved outcome-changing user decisions, normally one at a time.
Use current role-optimized Teamwork agents proactively, including GPT-5.6 Sol max Deep Judge/Reviewer for high-risk Codex review.
Map completion claims and Memory Delta status to direct evidence before accepting the result.
Update Teamwork version metadata, refresh installed skills/agents/global policy, and validate the installed surface.
